title: Wire ensureRulesFresh into 3 MCP tool handlers (R28 consumer)
description: Per D26 unified rule-access pattern: every MCP tool handler calling Fabric rules runs ensureRulesFresh first. Top-of-handler one-liner in tools/plan-context.ts, tools/get-rules.ts, tools/rule-sections.ts. RuleSyncReport.warnings injected into MCP response metadata for AI consumption (typed code + file:line + action_hint, R24 contract). Includes typed RuleError throws for validation failures (R11.3 — frontmatter parse, stable_id collision).
scope: Layer 2 P0 — R28 consumer at MCP entry points; merges R11.3 typed throws

files.change: Add at handler top: const syncReport = await ensureRulesFresh(projectRoot); merge syncReport.warnings into response.warnings
Throw RuleError (from FabricError taxonomy) on rule validation failures: invalid frontmatter / stable_id collision / content_ref_missing
NEW: assert tool handler calls ensureRulesFresh; warnings appear in response when rule has invalid frontmatter; RuleError surfaces via _error.ts as structured response

criteria: All 3 MCP tool handlers call ensureRulesFresh at start
RuleSyncReport.warnings merged into MCP response.warnings field
Invalid rule frontmatter throws RuleError with actionHint pointing to fab doctor --fix
Tool handler tests pass with mocked rule-sync
End-to-end test verifies AI consumer receives structured warning shape (typed code + file:line + action_hint)
